TimeEntry.Title: Cisco Umbrella Update on BWH-HQ-CONF-01 to Version 5.1.13.177
TimeEntry.Notes: Reviewed the Cisco Umbrella dashboard and identified that BWH-HQ-CONF-01 was running an outdated Umbrella client. Downloaded the latest version 5.1.13.177 , logged in on the backend, performed the upgrade, and confirmed successful installation. The dashboard now reflects the updated version, ensuring improved protection and policy enforcement.

TimeEntry.Title: Cisco Umbrella Update on BWH-HQ-IIS-01 to Version 5.1.13.177
TimeEntry.Notes: Reviewed the Cisco Umbrella dashboard and identified that BWH-HQ-IIS-01 was running an outdated Umbrella client. Downloaded the latest version 5.1.13.177 , logged in on the backend, performed the upgrade, and confirmed successful installation. The dashboard now reflects the updated version, ensuring improved protection and policy enforcement.

TimeEntry.Title: Cisco Umbrella Update on BWH-HQ-DT-02 to Version 5.1.13.177
TimeEntry.Notes: Reviewed the Cisco Umbrella dashboard and identified that BWH-HQ-DT-02 was running an outdated Umbrella client. Downloaded the latest version 5.1.13.177 , logged in on the backend, performed the upgrade, and confirmed successful installation. The dashboard now reflects the updated version, ensuring improved protection and policy enforcement.

TimeEntry.Title: Device Non-Compliant Due to Missing Patches- BWH-HQ-APP-01, BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01, BWH-HQ-VBR-01, BWH-HQ-VONE
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into myrmm portal and fond the following servers are non compliant due to missing patches. Download and install the missing patches on the servers and then rescan them on Myrmm portal. Verify the patches have been installed successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: QNAP firmware 5.2.7.3257 upgrade for BWH
TimeEntry.Notes: I logged into the QNAP system in the BWH environment and found that firmware updates were pending. Before proceeding with the update, I verified that all backup jobs had completed successfully. Since a reboot was required prior to installing the firmware, I rebooted the QNAP device, installed the updates, and then performed a second reboot to finalize the installation. After the system came back online, I verified that all shared folders were accessible. I then rescanned the backup repository, and the scan completed successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: Prepared the Windows 10 computer details for BWH as per Lead instructions.
TimeEntry.Notes: Prepared the Windows 10 computer inventory for BWH as per the instructions from Lead (Gurdeep) and sent the details to all leads via email.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.31.20309.0)updated on machine :- BWH-HQ-APP-01, BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01, BWH-HQ-WINEDGE, BWLAP-15
TimeEntry.Notes: Connected to the machines (BWH-HQ-APP-01, BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01, BWH-HQ-WINEDGE, BWLAP-15) and discovered they were running an older version of CrowdStrike. Attempted to push the sensor from the portal, but the process was unsuccessful.so connect to the system and uninstalled the older version, downloaded, and installed the latest sensor version (7.31.20309.0) on these machines. Verified the update through the portal. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.31.20309.0)updated on machines BWH-HQ-LT-08,BWH-HQ-DT-04,BWH-HQ-DT-02,BW-ACCT02
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines BWH-HQ-LT-08,BWH-HQ-DT-04,BWH-HQ-DT-02,BW-ACCT02
running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines and uninstalled the old version  and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version 7.31.20309.0
of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: For BWH, I don't think FS01 is used for anything. Can we power that down and remove our tools?
TimeEntry.Notes: As per Sunny instructions connected to the server and verified that there is no shared folder is shared from the server and powered it off for few days and then we will remove the tools.

TimeEntry.Title: Virtual Disk consolidation required - BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01  is on Disk consolidation. Go the snapshots and start consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully and then login to the server and verify all the services are up and working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.31.20309.0)updated on machine :- BWH-HQ-IIS-01, BWH-HQ-RDP-01, BWH-HQ-VBR-01, BWH-HQ-VONE
TimeEntry.Notes: Connected to the machines (BWH-HQ-IIS-01, BWH-HQ-RDP-01, BWH-HQ-VBR-01, BWH-HQ-VONE) and discovered they were running an older version of CrowdStrike. Attempted to push the sensor from the portal, but the process was unsuccessful.so connect to the system and uninstalled the older version, downloaded, and installed the latest sensor version (7.31.20309.0) on these machines. Verified the update through the portal. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.31.20309.0)updated on machines BWH-HQ-SQL-01,DC03,DC04,RA01
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines BWH-HQ-SQL-01,DC03,DC04,RA01
running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines and uninstalled the old version  and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version 7.31.20309.0
of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Identified Synchronization issues in ManageEngine BWH-HQ-VBR-01,BWH-HQ-APP-01,BWH-HQ-DT-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the ManageEngine portal and verified sync status for devices BWH-HQ-VBR-01,BWH-HQ-APP-01,BWH-HQ-DT-01.Checked communication between endpoint agents and the ManageEngine server.Performed troubleshooting steps to restore connectivity.Re-initiated synchronization and confirmed successful communication.Current Status:Synchronization has been successfully restored. All devices are now reporting and syncing properly in ManageEngine.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version (7.31.20309.0) updated on machines: BWH-HQ-LT-01, BWH-HQ-LT-13 and BWH-HQ-LT-03
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines  BWH-HQ-LT-01, BWH-HQ-LT-13 and BWH-HQ-LT-03 running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines and uninstalled the old version and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version  (7.31.20309.0) of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: Re:[## 5434 ##] Fw: Please review for your 2025/2026 informXL Analyzer Release
TimeEntry.Notes: Joined the meeting with informXL team and provided them access to the servers they have upgraded the database and application and tested on Pon machine and it was having the issue then t=the y made changes and its updated to latest version and working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Cisco Umbrella Update on BWH-HQ-LT-01, BWH-HQ-LT-06, BWWS017-PC, BWWS030-PC to Version 5.1.13.177
TimeEntry.Notes: Reviewed the Cisco Umbrella dashboard and identified that  BWH-HQ-LT-01, BWH-HQ-LT-06, BWWS017-PC, BWWS030-PC  were running an outdated Umbrella client. Downloaded the latest version 5.1.13.177 , logged in on the backend, performed the upgrade, and confirmed successful installation. The dashboard now reflects the updated version, ensuring improved protection and policy enforcement.

TimeEntry.Title: SERVER Alert - Attention - BWH-HQ-VBR-01: (10.1.1.35) - Memory Utilization is 82%, threshold value for this monitor is 75%
TimeEntry.Notes:
Worked on the assigned ticket. Logged into the OPS manager and observed machine BWH-HQ-VBR-01 has memory threshold error. So
Connected to machine from manage engine and killed the unnecessary task from task manager. After completing these steps,
 all sensors resumed normal operation. As a result, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.31.20309.0)updated on machines BWWS019-PC,BWWS016-PC, BWWS017-PC,BWLAP-06
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines BWWS019-PC,BWWS016-PC, BWWS017-PC,BWLAP-06
running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines and uninstalled the old version  and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version 7.31.20309.0
of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: InformXL upgrade call
TimeEntry.Notes: Call with Gurdeep and InformXL team.

TimeEntry.Title: BWH-HQ-LT-05: Re: My Computer
TimeEntry.Notes:
BWH-HQ-LT-05: Re: My Computer

While accessing your laptop, I noticed that the system was using a high amount of RAM (over 80% without any open app). I stopped unnecessary background services and optimized the system performance. The RAM usage is now below 50%.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Inform Excel not working
TimeEntry.Notes:
Received a report from Joon Kwon stating that Excel is not working properly after a recent update. The error message indicates an SQL Server connection failure and restricted content due to Trust Center settings.

Plan to connect remotely to the user’s machine to review and adjust the Trust Center settings in Excel. Settings from machine were good. Then connected to BWH-HQ-SQL-01 server, verified SQL services. Run all the SQL services. But still the issue was same. So discuss with Sunny and sent the email to InformXL support. Now waiting for their response. Worked with informXL support and now informXL is working on user machine, as confirmed by user.

TimeEntry.Title: TABLET-KLFSQ9HN : Critical and low disk space found over this machine.
TimeEntry.Notes: By following the standard cleanup procedure, the system’s free space has improved by up to 12 GB now.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Re: InformXL issue
TimeEntry.Notes:
Summary of Work with InformXL Support – Issue Resolved

  • Multiple users experienced SQL connection errors when launching InformXL after the recent upgrade.

  • You collaborated internally with Sourabh and Satish to review the issue and gather details before escalating.

  • You then worked directly with InformXL support (Dan Smilanick), sharing error information and coordinating a remote troubleshooting session.

  • InformXL support identified the root cause—a required update to a database table to reprioritize supported SQL drivers.

  • During the session, the InformXL team applied the needed update, and you verified functionality with Sourabh and Satish.

  • Users confirmed InformXL is now launching and connecting successfully.

  • Issue has been fully resolved.

TimeEntry.Title: Failed patches updated on machine BWH-HQ-CONF-01,BWH-HQ-LT-07,BWH-HQ-DT-03,BWH-HQ-LT-09,BWH-HQ-LT-08,BWH-HQ-LT-13
TimeEntry.Notes:
Worked on the  ticket. Login to the manage engine portal and checked failed or missing patches  for the below mentioned machine then download and installed 
the failed patches manually which are applicable to the computer. Then   scan the machine for installed patches.
After scan Check and verify the machine with the MY RMM portal. The failed patches are updated successfully and machine are in healthy state.


Machine :  BWH-HQ-CONF-01,BWH-HQ-LT-07,BWH-HQ-DT-03,BWH-HQ-LT-09,BWH-HQ-LT-08,BWH-HQ-LT-13


Patch installed:
Patch ID   Patch Name
112875 Update for Microsoft WebView2 Runtime (x64) (143.0.3650.66)
43061  Microsoft Edge for chromium business (143.0.3650.66) (x64)
353894 Adobe Acrobat Reader DC (x64) (25.001.20982)

TimeEntry.Title: Weekly Firewall backup for -BWH
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the firewall to perform the weekly backup for the [BWH] firewall, copy backup files, and place them in the Team Channel under the network config-firewall folder. Hence, closing this ticket

TimeEntry.Title: HELP
TimeEntry.Notes:
Received a email from Rosa Torres stating that Excel is not working properly after a recent update. The error message indicates an SQL Server connection failure and restricted content due to Trust Center settings.

Connected remotely to the user’s machine to review and adjust the Trust Center settings in Excel. Settings from machine were good. Then connected to BWH-HQ-SQL-01 server, verified SQL services. Run all the SQL services. But still the issue was same. So discuss with Sunny and sent the email to InformXL support. Now waiting for their response. Worked with informXL support and now informXL is working on user machine, as confirmed by user.

TimeEntry.Title: ManageEngine Agent Syncing Issue with the Portal on These Machines: - BWH-HQ-VONE, BWH-HQ-WINEDGE, BWWS022-PC
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into Manage engine portal and found the agent on machines BWH-HQ-VONE, BWH-HQ-WINEDGE, BWWS022-PC not communication with portal. So connected to the machine, tried to restart the service, but nothing happened. So, Uninstall the Corrupt manage engine then download and the latest manage engine agent. After that agent on machine is communicating with the portal and working fine hence closing this ticket

TimeEntry.Title: Snapshot consolidation required - BWH-HQ-RDP-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server BWH-HQ-RDP-01 is on Disk consolidation. Go the snapshots and start consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully and then login to the server and verify all the services are up and working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: VMwareTool version (VMware tools 13 (x64) (13.0.5) upgrade on Servers
TimeEntry.Notes:
VMwareTool version (VMware tools 13 (x64) (13.0.5) upgrade on Servers BWH-HQ-APP-01, BWH-HQ-IIS-01, BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01, BWH-HQ-RDP-01, BWH-HQ-SQL-01, BWH-HQ-VBR-01

Logged into each server and added CD/DVD, Mounted disk and then installed VMware tool Version ((VMware tools 13 (x64) (13.0.5), server rebooted and verified Latest version updated.

TimeEntry.Title: ManageEngine Agent Communication Issue with Portal on the Following :BWLAP-06, BWWS017-PC, BWWS030-PC
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the ManageEngine portal and found that the agents on BWLAP-06, BWWS017-PC, BWWS030-PC were not communicating with the console. Connected to each affected system and attempted to restart the ManageEngine service, but it failed to respond. Uninstalled the corrupted ManageEngine agent and installed the latest version. After reinstallation, all agents successfully reconnected and are now communicating properly with the portal. Issue resolved and ticket closed.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.31.20309.0)updated on machines BWH-CABRILLO-TV,BWH-HQ-CONF-01,BWH-HQ-DT-01,BWH-HQ-DT-03
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines BWH-CABRILLO-TV,BWH-HQ-CONF-01,BWH-HQ-DT-01,BWH-HQ-DT-03
running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines and uninstalled the old version  and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version 7.31.20309.0
of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.31.20309.0)updated on machine :- BWH-HQ-LT-06, BWH-HQ-LT-07, BWH-HQ-LT-09, BWH-HQ-LT-10
TimeEntry.Notes: Connected to the machines (BWH-HQ-LT-06, BWH-HQ-LT-07, BWH-HQ-LT-09, BWH-HQ-LT-10) and discovered they were running an older version of CrowdStrike. Attempted to push the sensor from the portal, but the process was unsuccessful.so connect to the system and uninstalled the older version, downloaded, and installed the latest sensor version (7.31.20309.0) on these machines. Verified the update through the portal. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: One Drive
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary – OneDrive Not Running / Unable to Download Files

Issue:
User reported that OneDrive was not running, which prevented them from downloading files.

Actions Taken:

  • Connected to the user’s computer for live troubleshooting.

  • Verified that OneDrive was not properly syncing.

  • Reconfigured the OneDrive client and ensured the user was signed into the correct Microsoft 365 account.

  • Confirmed that the OneDrive app was running, syncing, and connected to the cloud.

  • Performed a test file download/upload to confirm functionality.

Status:
? OneDrive successfully reconfigured
? Syncing and downloads working correctly
? User confirmed full access and functionality

Next Steps:
No further action required unless the issue returns.

TimeEntry.Title: inform XL blocked
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary – Excel File Blocked (Macros Disabled Due to Security Risk)

Issue:
User reported that an Excel workbook was showing the warning:
“SECURITY RISK: Microsoft has blocked macros from running because the source of this file is untrusted.”
The user needed urgent assistance to open the file and enable full functionality.

Actions Taken:

  • Connected remotely to the user’s machine for live troubleshooting.

  • Identified the file path and all related workbook dependencies.

  • Verified that the file was being blocked due to Microsoft’s security controls for files downloaded from external/untrusted sources.

  • Opened Excel Trust Center and added the specific file path (and related directory) to Trusted Locations.

  • Ensured macros and workbook links were now allowed to run safely.

  • Re-opened the workbook to verify functionality—no security warnings appeared.

  • Performed a test of all affected sheets and calculations.

  • User confirmed the Excel file was now fully accessible and functioning as expected.

Status:
? Trusted Location added successfully
? Macros and workbook functionality restored
? User confirmed resolution
? Ticket closed

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Vpn help asap
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary – VPN Not Working / SSL Certificate Expired / Users Disconnected

Issue:
User reported issues accessing the VPN. Sunny requested immediate assistance for Traci, whose husband was also unable to connect. Initial investigation showed the VPN portal was not loading.

Root Cause Identified:

  • The SSL certificate for the VPN portal had expired, causing VPN access failures.

?? Actions Taken

1. Initial Troubleshooting

  • Connected remotely to the user’s system to verify the issue.

  • Confirmed that the VPN portal was failing to load due to an expired SSL certificate.

  • Escalated internally for SSL renewal.

2. SSL Certificate Updated

  • Once the SSL certificate was renewed, all users connected to the VPN were automatically disconnected, which triggered additional issues.

3. Further Investigation with Sophos Support

  • Connected with Sophos Support to review logs and diagnose the widespread disconnections.

  • After reviewing firewall and VPN logs, it was determined that the VPN software package needed to be reinstalled on affected machines.

4. Resolution Work

  • Reinstalled the VPN package as required and restored proper communication with the firewall.

  • Verified tunnel stability and confirmed successful authentication after reinstall.

5. User Reconfiguration & Validation

  • Connected with Traci and fully reconfigured the VPN client.

  • Confirmed that Traci was able to connect successfully after the reinstall.

  • Connected with Rosa and performed the same VPN setup, configuration, and validation.

  • Ensured both users could connect without errors and confirmed stable VPN connectivity.

TimeEntry.Title: VMwareTool version (VMware tools 13 (x64) (13.0.5) upgrade on Servers
TimeEntry.Notes:
VMwareTool version (VMware tools 13 (x64) (13.0.5) upgrade on Servers


VMwareTool version (VMware tools 13 (x64) (13.0.5) upgrade on Servers BWH-HQ-VONE, BWH-HQ-WINEDGE, DC03, DC04, FS01, RA01, RG01


Logged into each server and added CD/DVD, Mounted disk and then installed VMware tool Version ((VMware tools 13 (x64) (13.0.5), server rebooted and verified Latest version updated.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version updated 7.32.20403.0 on BWH-HQ-DT-02 & BWH-HQ-DT-04
TimeEntry.Notes: After checking the CrowdStrike portal, I found that  Crowdstrike latest version updatedBWH-HQ-DT-02 & BWH-HQ-DT-04 needed updates. I updated both to version 7.32.20403.0

TimeEntry.Title: BWLAP-06 : Critical and low disk space found here (Only 1.4GB remains)
TimeEntry.Notes: I accessed the user’s computer and followed the standard procedure to clear temporary folders, remove old patches, empty the Recycle Bin, and disable hibernation. As a result, the system’s free space has improved to 28 GB approx today.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.32.20403.0)updated on machine :- BWWS016-PC, BWWS021-PC, BWWS029-PC, BWWS-CONF2
TimeEntry.Notes: Connected to the machines (BWWS016-PC, BWWS021-PC, BWWS029-PC, BWWS-CONF2)and discovered they were running an older version of CrowdStrike. Attempted to push the sensor from the portal, but the process was unsuccessful.so connect to the system through MY Rmm and uninstalled the older version, downloaded, and installed the latest sensor version (7.32.20403.0) on these machines. Verified the update through the portal. Hence, closing this ticket.

Login into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines BWLAP-19,BWWS017-PC,BWLAP-06,BWH-HQ-LT-15
running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines and uninstalled the old version  and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version 7.32.20403.0
of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Snapshot consolidation required - BWH-HQ-IIS-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server BWH-HQ-IIS-01 is on Disk consolidation. Go the snapshots and start consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully and then login to the server and verify all the services are up and working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Server BWH-HQ-IIS-01,BWH-HQ-RDP-01,BWH-HQ-VONE,DC04,DC03 pending for reboot after APD
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login in manage engine portal and checked  machines . Found below mentioned server pending for reboot after APD. So login into the servers, and restart the server.
After reboot login into the server, and checked required services are running. Then login again in manage engine portal and scan the machines for installed patches in APD. After scan  the machines are now in healthy systems.
Machine  : BWH-HQ-IIS-01,BWH-HQ-RDP-01,BWH-HQ-VONE,DC04,DC03

TimeEntry.Title: Server Restarted After APD to Deploy Patches (BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01, BWH-HQ-SQL-01 & BWH-HQ-APP-01)
TimeEntry.Notes:
Performed the APD on the servers as part of the scheduled patch deployment. Restarted BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01,BWH-HQ-SQL-01,& BWH-HQ-APP-01after applying the patches. After the restart, verified all automatic services and confirmed they are running as expected.


Following the verification, initiated a full scan from the ManageEngine portal to update the patch status and clear any pending items from the console. All servers are now reporting correctly in the portal.

TimeEntry.Title: Update failed patches on BWH-HQ-DT-02
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per patch management, checked the failed patches on the below computer and found updates required. Updated the patch and performed a manual scan in the ManageEngine portal.

BWH-HQ-DT-02:

  • Precision 5860 Tower (Version 3.3.0)

Patch updation completed and the system is now compliant.

TimeEntry.Title: BWH-HQ-LT-13 has Missing patch while last run
TimeEntry.Notes:
I worked on the Missing patches and logged into the ManageEngine portal to review the approved patches for the machines. I downloaded and manually installed the applicable failed patches, then rebooted Also scanned the machines. Some patches not applicable due to non compatibility. All previously missing patches were successfully updated.
Machines are BWH-HQ-LT-13

Patches are Zoom Workplace (EXE) (x64) (User Based) (6.6.11.23272)

After reboot login into the server, and checked required services are running. Then login again in manage engine portal and scan the machine for installed patches in APD. After scan  the machine is now in healthy systems.


Machine : BWH-HQ-VBR-01

TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the firewall to perform the weekly backup for the Sophos Firewall , copy backup files, and place them in the Team Channel under the network config-firewall folder. Hence, closing this ticket

After that agent on machine is communicating with the portal and working fine hence closing this ticket

TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines DC04, RA01, RG0 running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines from backend and uninstalled the old version and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version 7.32.20403.0 of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Screenconnect Latest version (v 25.8.3.9466) update:- BWH-CABRILLO-TV, BWH-HQ-LT-07, BWLAP-06, BWLAP-18
TimeEntry.Notes: Checked and found that the devices (BWH-CABRILLO-TV, BWH-HQ-LT-07, BWLAP-06, BWLAP-18) were running an outdated version of Screen Connect. I initiated the update for these devices from the portal, but after the update, I noticed some issues when verifying the version on the portal. connected to the machines through My Rmm and uninstall the screen connect software . after that download and install the fresh MyRemote setup and install on it  and verify that agents have been updated successfully with the latest version(v 25.8.3.9466).). Hence closing the ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Screenconnect Latest version (v 25.8.3.9466) updated on machine BWWS016-PC, BWWS017-PC, BWWS021-PC & BWWS029-PC
TimeEntry.Notes: Checked and found that the computers machine  BWWS016-PC, BWWS017-PC, BWWS021-PC & BWWS029-PC were running an old version of ScreenConnect. update the software from the portal to the new version 25.8.3.9466. After updating, verify that the version on the portal has been successfully updated to the latest version. Hence Closing this ticket

TimeEntry.Title: BWLAP-15 : Re: Computer issues
TimeEntry.Notes: At the moment, during the remote session, everything appears to be functioning properly, and the processor temperature is within an acceptable range. For a deeper diagnosis, extended testing may be required, which could take a longer duration and would need to be performed after business hours. An email has been sent to the user requesting consent; however, we have not yet received confirmation from their side.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: One Drive
TimeEntry.Notes:
User reported an issue regarding the OneDrive...

so connected to the computer and checked the OneDrive...

it was fuly sync.....asked the user to  check the OneDrive...

user checked and confirmed its working fine.....no more issue reported by the user......hence closing the ticket....

TimeEntry.Title: BWLAP-15 : Stil having freeze and reboot issue
TimeEntry.Notes: I have completed a full diagnostic and deep scan of your machine. Some services that were consuming system resources have been disabled. Let’s monitor the system over the next day to see if these changes improve performance.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Your veeam license has been expired on BWH-HQ-VONE-01
TimeEntry.Notes: During the Veeam backup check, I noticed that the VSPC portal was showing a warning that the Veeam license had expired. To resolve this, I connected to the BWH-HQ-VONE-01 server and verified that the license was running in the grace period. I then logged into the Veeam Pulse portal, updated the license expiry date to the end of March, downloaded the required license file, and initiated the installation process. Once the installation was completed, I restarted the VSPC agent service on the server and verified that the updated license is now visible on the VSPC portal.

TimeEntry.Title: BWLAP-15 : Stil having freeze and reboot issue
TimeEntry.Notes:
Monitored system BWLAP15 and identified excessive temporary files and patch installer files.

Safely deleted unnecessary temp files and patch-related installers to free up disk space.

During monitoring, confirmed the system did not reboot automatically.

User confirmation is pending to verify system behavior and stability.

Recycle Bin is currently full; user will be contacted to confirm approval for cleanup.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.32.20403.0)updated on machines BWH-CABRILLO-TV, BWH-HQ-APP-01, BWLAP-15, BWLAP-18
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines BWH-CABRILLO-TV, BWH-HQ-APP-01, BWLAP-15, BWLAP-18 running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines from backend and uninstalled the old version and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version 7.32.20403.0 of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Screenconnect Latest version (v 25.8.3.9466) update:- BWH-HQ-DT-03, BWH-HQ-DT-02, BWH-HQ-DT-01, BWH-HQ-CONF-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Checked and found that the devices (BWH-HQ-DT-03, BWH-HQ-DT-02, BWH-HQ-DT-01, BWH-HQ-CONF-01) were running an outdated version of Screen Connect. I initiated the update for these devices from the portal, but after the update, I noticed some issues when verifying the version on the portal. connected to the machines through My Rmm and uninstall the screen connect software through Revo uninstaller. after that download and install the fresh MyRemote setup and install on it  and verify that agents have been updated successfully with the latest version(v 25.8.3.9466).). Hence closing the ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Site was down
TimeEntry.Notes:
The entire BWH site went down, as reported by Saule. I verified that the firewall was not accessible. I then contacted Cox Business, and they confirmed that connectivity was present at the customer end. Since no IT personnel were available onsite, I asked Sai to visit the location for further troubleshooting.

During the visit, I confirmed with screenshots that Cox Business connectivity was up and all signals were normal. I then asked Sai to connect to their network and try to access the firewall, but it was still inaccessible. I instructed him to reboot the firewall once; when that did not resolve the issue, we tried rebooting it a second time, but the issue persisted.

Next, I asked Sai to connect a monitor to the MCP box and access it from a laptop. We found that it was stuck, possibly due to power fluctuations. I brought the MCP box back up, and gradually all the servers started coming online. The site is now up and fully operational.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: BWH visit
TimeEntry.Notes: An internet outage was reported, and I conducted an on-site visit after the client previously noted excessive noise coming from the network switches. Initial diagnostics confirmed that the switches were functioning properly and were not the source of the issue. Further inspection revealed that the firewall power supply cable was not securely connected and became disconnected when the appliance was touched. Although the wireless network was visible, there was no internet connectivity. Multiple troubleshooting steps were performed, including assigning different IP addresses and directly connecting the Cox Ethernet line to a laptop, which also failed to establish connectivity. Due to the unexpected power interruption, the ESXi virtual machines did not automatically restart. While the physical hardware and MCB were powered on, the firewall virtual machine and related services were down. All affected virtual machines were manually started, network services were fully restored, and functionality was verified before leaving the site.

TimeEntry.Title: Site was down
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary – Site Outage / Firewall Verification

Worked with SAI during a full site outage. Verified existing network rules and reviewed Sophos firewall configuration. Identified misconfiguration impacting site connectivity. Updated and corrected firewall and validated network flow.

After changes, tested connectivity and confirmed all services restored. Site was successfully brought back online and verified operational.

TimeEntry.Title: Screenconnect Latest version (v 25.8.3.9466) update:- BWH-HQ-LT-13, BWH-HQ-LT-09, BWWS016-PC, BWWS022-PC
TimeEntry.Notes: Checked and found that the devices (BWH-HQ-LT-13, BWH-HQ-LT-09, BWWS016-PC, BWWS022-PC) were running an outdated version of Screen Connect. I initiated the update for these devices from the portal, but after the update, I noticed some issues when verifying the version on the portal. connected to the machines through My Rmm and uninstall the screen connect software through Revo uninstaller. after that download and install the fresh MyRemote setup and install on it  and verify that agents have been updated successfully with the latest version(v 25.8.3.9466).). Hence closing the ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version updated 7.32.20403.0 on BWH-HQ-LT-05,BWH-HQ-LT-19 & BWH-HQ-LT-07
TimeEntry.Notes: Accessed the CrowdStrike portal and identified that BWH-HQ-LT-05,BWH-HQ-LT-19 & BWH-HQ-LT-07 required an update to the latest version. Subsequently, I updated all to version 7.32.20403.0

TimeEntry.Title: QNAP firmware 5.2.8.3332 upgrade for BWH
TimeEntry.Notes: I logged into the QNAP system in the BWH environment and found that firmware updates were pending. Before proceeding with the update, I verified that all backup jobs had completed successfully. Since a reboot was required prior to installing the firmware, I rebooted the QNAP device, installed the updates, and then performed a second reboot to finalize the installation. After the system came back online, I verified that all shared folders were accessible. I then rescanned the backup repository, and the scan completed successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.32.20403.0)updated on machines BWH-HQ-IIS-01, BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01, BWH-HQ-RDP-01, BWH-HQ-SQL-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the ManageEngine portal and found that the agents on BWLAP-06, BWWS017-PC, BWWS030-PC were not communicating with the console. Connected to each affected system and attempted to restart the ManageEngine service, but it failed to respond. Uninstalled the corrupted ManageEngine agent and installed the latest version. After reinstallation, all agents successfully reconnected and are now communicating properly with the portal. Issue resolved and ticket closed.

TimeEntry.Title: Pending Patches Updated on BWLAP-06,BWH-HQ-SQL-01 & BWH-HQ-LT-09 Systems
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per patch management, we checked the pending patches on the computers listed below and identified required updates. The patches were installed one by one, followed by a manual scan in the ManageEngine portal

BWLAP-06,BWH-HQ-SQL-01 & BWH-HQ-LT-09

On-premises data gateway (3000.298.7.0)
Update for Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise Current Channel for x64 2511 of version(19426.20218)

All previously missing patches were successfully updated.Machines are DC03,DC04 , Patches are The latest update for Microsoft Defender (1.443.237.0)

So connected to machines and uninstalled the old version and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version (7.32.20403.0) of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Weekly Firewall Backup – Sophos
TimeEntry.Notes: Summary: Accessed the BWH Sophos firewall and performed the weekly configuration backup. The backup file was downloaded and saved in the Teams channel under Network Config → Firewall.

TimeEntry.Title: Server BWH-HQ-IIS-01,BWH-HQ-RDP-01,BWH-HQ-APP-01,BWH-HQ-SQL-01 pending for reboot after APD
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login in manage engine portal and checked  machines . Found below mentioned server pending for reboot after APD. So login into the servers, and restart the server.
After reboot login into the server, and checked required services are running. Then login again in manage engine portal and scan the machines for installed patches in APD. After scan  the machines are now in healthy systems.
Machine  : BWH-HQ-IIS-01,BWH-HQ-RDP-01,BWH-HQ-APP-01,BWH-HQ-SQL-01

TimeEntry.Title: Please help - I can't access to mirasol@brandywine-homes.com anymore
TimeEntry.Notes:
Myle reported an issue regarding the mailbox...

So connected to the computer and checked user is not able to log into the account....

so asked user to type the password and it worked fine....However user is getting the Authentication code...

so login to the Azure portal and remove the authenticator and she is able to login...

hence closing the ticket....

I initiated the update for these devices from the portal, but after the update, I noticed some issues when verifying the version on the portal. connected to the machines through My Rmm and uninstall the screen connect software through Revo uninstaller. after that download and install the fresh MyRemote setup and install on it  and verify that agents have been updated successfully with the latest version(v 25.9.5.9483).). Hence closing the ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Screenconnect version upgraded to latest release- 25.9.5.9483 on servers.
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the Myremote server and then take a screenshot of the server for safe revert if required. After that download and install the latest version on screenconnect on the server. Once the version has been upgraded then verify all services and access on the sceenconnect. Once everything looks fine then push the version upgrade to all the servers for BWH and verify them on portal. with password push and other utilities.

TimeEntry.Title: Screenconnect version upgraded to latest release- 25.9.5.9483 on BWH-HQ-CONF-01, BWH-HQ-DT-03, BWH-HQ-LT-01, BWH-HQ-LT-06, BWH-HQ-LT-13
TimeEntry.Notes: Myremote version upgrade to Latest Version -25.9.5.9483  Pushed the agent from portal but no luck so then connected to below server and uninstalled old version (25.8.3.9466) of myremote and installed the latest version on them, verified all VMs are up and running in myremote now. BWH-HQ-CONF-01, BWH-HQ-DT-03, BWH-HQ-LT-01, BWH-CABRILLO-TV, BWH-HQ-LT-13

TimeEntry.Title: Re: NEWSTAR - Need LIVE to PROTO copy done
TimeEntry.Notes:
Completed LIVE to PROTO database copy successfully during after-hours as approved to avoid any impact on business operations.

Details:

  • All prerequisites were verified prior to starting the process.

  • The DB Copy procedure PDF was followed for all steps to ensure accuracy and compliance.

  • LIVE database backup was taken and restored to PROTO.

  • Existing PROTO database was dropped and replaced with LIVE data.

  • Database copy completed successfully with no errors.

  • PROTO database services restarted and confirmed running.

Status:
? LIVE to PROTO data copy completed successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version updated 7.32.20403.0 on BWWS019-PC & BWH-HQ-LT-18
TimeEntry.Notes: After checking the CrowdStrike portal, I found that  Crowdstrike latest version updated  JBWWS019-PC & BWH-HQ-LT-18  needed updates. I updated both to version 7.32.20403.0

TimeEntry.Title: SERVER Alert - Attention - BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01: (10.1.1.113) - Memory Utilization is 77%, threshold value for this monitor is 75%
TimeEntry.Notes:
Worked on the assigned ticket. Logged into the OPS manager and observed machine BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01 has memory threshold error. So
Connected to machine from manage engine and killed the unnecessary task from task manager. After completing these steps,
 all sensors resumed normal operation. As a result, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: MyRMM Manage-Engine Latest version 11.4.2528.25.W updated:- BWWS016-PC, BWWS021-PC, BWWS029-PC, BWWS-CONF2
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the portal, Found New Agent Version (11.4.2528.25.W) was released, and Pushed the sensor from the portal but it failed so connected to the below machine (BWWS016-PC, BWWS021-PC, BWWS029-PC, BWWS-CONF2) from Manage Engine, Tried to update the version but got an error (old version is already installed on it) so removed the earlier installed version. Downloaded and installed the new version after verifying the same from the portal, Devices are up to date with the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Manage engine version upgraded to latest version 11.4.2528.25.W
TimeEntry.Notes:
Logged into the server and took a snapshot of the MyRMM server as a precaution for rollback if required. After that, downloaded and installed the latest version of MyRMM on the server. Once the upgrade was completed, verified all services and confirmed access in the ManageEngine portal.

After confirming everything was functioning correctly, pushed the version upgrade to all BWH servers and verified their status in the portal, including password push and other related utilities.

TimeEntry.Title: Virtual Disk consolidation required - BWH-HQ-SQL-01.
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server BWH-HQ-SQL-01 is on Disk consolidation. Go the snapshots and start consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully and then login to the server and verify all the services are up and working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Identified ManageEngine Sync Issue –BWH-HQ-WINEDGE,BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01,BWH-HQ-VONE,BWWS016-PC
TimeEntry.Notes: Identified a syncing issue in ManageEngine for the devices  The systems were not properly synchronizing with the ManageEngine server, causing delays in reporting and updates.Actions Taken:Logged into the ManageEngine portal and verified the sync status for affected machines BWH-HQ-WINEDGE,BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01,BWH-HQ-VONE,BWWS016-PC.Checked communication between the endpoint agent and the ManageEngine server.Performed necessary troubleshooting steps to re-establish connectivity and resolve synchronization errors.Re-initiated sync and confirmed successful communication.Verified in ManageEngine that both devices are now syncing properly and reporting as expected.Current Status:The sync issue has been successfully resolved. All devices are now syncing properly in ManageEngine.

TimeEntry.Title: Veeam ONE Server upgraded on 13.0.1.5924 on the BWH-HQ-VONE
TimeEntry.Notes: During the Veeam backup check, I noticed that the VSPC portal indicated a new update was available for Veeam ONE Server, upgrading from version 13.0.0.5630 to 13.0.1.5924 for BWH-HQ-VONE. To proceed, I connected to the BWH-HQ-VONE server and verified the settings. Next, I logged into vCenter, took a snapshot of the server for safety, downloaded the required ISO file, and initiated the upgrade process. After the upgrade was completed, I rebooted the server and confirmed that all services were running properly. Finally, I verified that the Veeam ONE application was functioning correctly and is now visible on the VSPC portal.

TimeEntry.Title: SERVER Alert - Trouble - BWH-HQ-VONE: (10.1.1.19) - Disk Utilization is 65%, threshold value for this monitor is 60%
TimeEntry.Notes: As per the alert connected to BWH-HQ-VONE, download and install TreeSize, a disk space management tool that provides a detailed overview of the disk usage across folders and files on the system. After installation, TreeSize was run to analyse the disk. TreeSize scans the system and generates a report showing the size of all folders and files in a hierarchical structure so carefully review all folders and files occupying excessive space. Based on the review, we proceeded to clear unnecessary files that were identified as no longer needed. This included: Temporary Files: Files in the system's temporary folder are often created by applications during installation, updates, or other processes; clear these temporary files to free up space. Recycle Bin: emptied the Recycle Bin, where deleted files are temporarily stored. This ensured that deleted files were permanently removed from the disk and no longer took up space. Downloaded Items: System error memory dump files, Old Windows updates, previous Windows installations other system cache files. 44% of the space is free. Now Sufficient Free Space Available.

TimeEntry.Title: TABLET-KLFSQ9HN : Critical and low disk space found over this pc
TimeEntry.Notes: I accessed the machine and improved disk space by up to 13 GB by following the standard cleaning procedure.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: ManageEngine Agent Communication Issue with Portal on the Following :DC03, DC04, RA01, RG01
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the ManageEngine portal and found that the agents on DC03, DC04, RA01, RG01 were not communicating with the console. Connected to each affected system and attempted to restart the ManageEngine service, but it failed to respond. Uninstalled the corrupted ManageEngine agent and installed the latest version. After reinstallation, all agents successfully reconnected and are now communicating properly with the portal. Issue resolved and ticket closed.

TimeEntry.Title: Update failed patches on the (BWWS021-PC, BWH-HQ-DT-01, BWWS029-PC, BWH-HQ-DT-02, BWH-HQ-DT-04, BWH-HQ-LT-06:)
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per patch management, checked the pending patches for the below computers and found multiple driver updates required. Connected to each system and updated the patches one by one, including network, audio, and chipset-related drivers. Systems were restarted where required to complete the installations, and a manual scan was performed in the ManageEngine portal to clear the pending patch status and confirm compliance.

BWWS021-PC, BWH-HQ-DT-01, BWWS029-PC, BWH-HQ-DT-02, BWH-HQ-DT-04, BWH-HQ-LT-06:

  • Patch ID 1318779: Raptor Lake PCH CNVi WiFi
    [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_51F1] – Version 23.170.0.1

  • Patch ID 1318640: Realtek High Definition Audio
    [HDAUDIO\FUNC_01&VEN_10EC&DEV_0236] – Version 6.0.9879.1

  • Patch ID 1318782: Intel Wireless-AC 9560
    [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_7AF0] – Version 23.170.0.1

  • Patch ID 1318986: Intel Ethernet Connection (17) I219-LM
    [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_1A1C] – Version 12.19.2.65

  • Patch ID 1318783: Meteor Lake PCH CNVi WiFi
    [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_7E40] – Version 23.170.0.1

  • Patch ID 1318902: RTL8153 Gigabit Ethernet Adapter
    [USB\VID_0BDA&PID_8153] – Version 1153.20.805.2025

  • Patch ID 1318648: Realtek High Definition Audio
    [INTELAUDIO\FUNC_01&VEN_10EC&DEV_0236] – Version 6.0.9879.1

All required patches were installed successfully. Systems were restarted as needed, and post-deployment scans confirm that all listed computers are now compliant in the ManageEngine portal.

TimeEntry.Title: Weekly Firewall backup for the BWH Firewall
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the firewall to perform the weekly backup for the BWH Firewall, downloaded the backup file, and placed it in the Team Channel under the network config-firewall folder. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Notes: Login into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines BWH-HQ-DT-03 and BWH-HQ-DT-04 running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines and uninstalled the old version and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version  (7.32.20403.0) of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. TimeEntry.Title: RG01 server didn't reboot from last 111 Days
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into manage engine portal and found the server RG01 didn't reboot from last 111 days. So connected to the server and reboot the server. After reboot, verified all the required services are running.

TimeEntry.Title: Update failed patches on the BWWS017-PC,BWH-HQ-LT-01
TimeEntry.Notes:
As part of patch management activities, reviewed and addressed the failed patches on the below systems. Connected to each machine, installed the required driver updates, and restarted the systems where necessary to complete the patch installation. After reboot, a manual rescan was performed from the patch management portal to verify successful deployment and compliance.

BWWS017-PC, BWH-HQ-LT-01:

  • RTL8153 Gigabit Ethernet Adapter
    [USB\VID_0BDA&PID_8153] – Version 1153.20.805.2025

  • Raptor Lake PCH CNVi WiFi
    [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_51F1] – Version 23.170.0.1

  • Realtek High Definition Audio
    [INTELAUDIO\FUNC_01&VEN_10EC&DEV_0289] – Version 6.0.9879.1

  • Ethernet Connection (7) I219-LM
    [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_15BB] – Version 12.19.2.65

All listed patches were successfully updated, systems rebooted as required, and the patch status is now showing as compliant in the portal.

TimeEntry.Title: MyRMM Manage-Engine Latest version 11.4.2528.25.W updated:- BWH-HQ-DT-01, BWH-HQ-DT-02, BWH-HQ-LT-09, BWH-HQ-LT-06
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the portal, Found New Agent Version (11.4.2528.25.W) was released, and Pushed the sensor from the portal but it failed so connected to the below machine (BWH-HQ-DT-01, BWH-HQ-DT-02, BWH-HQ-LT-09, BWH-HQ-LT-06), Tried to update the version but got an error (old version is already installed on it) so removed the earlier installed version. Downloaded and installed the new version after verifying the same from the portal, Devices are up to date with the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Manage-Engine Latest version updated 11.4.2528.25.W - BWH-HQ-APP-01,BWH-HQ-IIS-01,BWH-HQ-RDP-01,BWH-HQ-SQL-01
TimeEntry.Notes:
Review the My RMM portal, and found the machine BWH-HQ-APP-01,BWH-HQ-IIS-01,BWH-HQ-RDP-01,BWH-HQ-SQL-01 running with old version of 
Manage-Engine. So connected to the machine  and then uninstalled the OLD version and downloaded and installed the latest
version (11.4.2528.25.W) of the manage-engine agent. Verified from the portal, device is running with updated agent version.

TimeEntry.Title: Communication with VSPC central server having issue on VONE server.
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into BWH-HQ-VONE server and found the server is not communicating with central VSPC server. Troubleshoot the issue with agent and then apply few articles to fix the issue. Setup the connection again and then clear the error on server. After that verify all alarms on server.

TimeEntry.Title: Manage-Engine Latest version 11.4.2528.25.W updated on BW-ACCT02,BWH-HQ-LT-05 & BWH-HQ-LT-07
TimeEntry.Notes: Reviewed the RMM portal and identified that the   BW-ACCT02,BWH-HQ-LT-05 & BWH-HQ-LT-07  were running an outdated version of ManageEngine.  downloaded and installed the latest version (11.4.2528.25.W) of the ManageEngine agent. scan the system with the  portal that the device now runs the updated agent version

TimeEntry.Title: SERVER Alert - Attention - BWH-HQ-VONE: (10.1.1.19) - CPU Utilization is 85%, threshold value for this monitor is 70%
TimeEntry.Notes:
Worked on the assigned ticket. Logged into the OPS manager and observed machine BWH-HQ-VONE has CPU threshold error. So
Connected to machine from manage engine and killed the unnecessary task from task manager. After completing these steps,
 all sensors resumed normal operation. As a result, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: SERVER Alert - Attention - BWH-HQ-VBR-01: (10.1.1.35) - Memory Utilization is 83%, threshold value for this monitor is 75%
TimeEntry.Notes:
Worked on the assigned ticket. Logged into the OPS manager and observed machine BWH-HQ-VBR-01 has memory threshold error. So
Connected to machine from manage engine and killed the unnecessary task from task manager. After completing these steps,
 all sensors resumed normal operation. As a result, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: MyRMM Manage-Engine Latest version 11.4.2528.25.W updated:- BWH-HQ-DT-03, BWH-HQ-LT-07, BWH-HQ-LT-13, BWLAP-19
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the portal, Found New Agent Version (11.4.2528.25.W) was released, and Pushed the sensor from the portal but it failed so connected to the below machine (BWH-HQ-DT-03, BWH-HQ-LT-07, BWH-HQ-LT-13, BWLAP-19)Tried to update the version but got an error (old version is already installed on it) so removed the earlier installed version. Downloaded and installed the new version after verifying the same from the portal, Devices are up to date with the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: My Remote version 25.9.5.9483 updated on machine RG01,RA01,DC04,DC03
TimeEntry.Notes:
Review the my remote portal, and found  the machine RG01,RA01,DC04,DC03
running with old version of My Remote. Tried to reinstall the agent from my remote portal, but not succeeded. Tried to restart the service and reinstall again, but unable to update it. So connected to the machine from manage engine  .
Uninstalled the old version and reinstalled the latest version 25.9.5.9483. After that refresh the machine from the portal. It was updated to latest
version of My Remote. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Screenconnect Latest version (v 25.9.5.9483) update:- BWH-HQ-DT-01 and BWH-HQ-LT-07
TimeEntry.Notes: Review the my remote portal, and found the machine BWH-HQ-DT-01 and BWH-HQ-LT-07 running with old version of My Remote. Tried to reinstall the agent from my remote portal, but not succeeded. Tried to restart the service and reinstall again, but unable to update it. So connected to the machine from manage engine .Uninstalled the old version and reinstalled the latest version 25.9.5.9483. After that refresh the machine from the portal. It was updated to latestversion of My Remote. Hence closing this ticket.
